paper_title: Clinical Diagnosis and Cross-Reactivity in Oral Allergy Syndrome

methods.method_name: Clinical Diagnosis of Oral Allergy Syndrome
methods.description: Process for diagnosing oral allergy syndrome based on reported symptoms and cross-reactivity between pollen and food allergens.

steps.description: Collect patient history and reported symptoms related to oral allergy syndrome.
steps.searchable_summary: gather patient history and symptoms for oral allergy syndrome

steps.description: Analyze the collected data to identify patterns of cross-reactivity between pollen and food allergens.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze symptom data for cross-reactivity patterns

steps.description: Validate the diagnosis by comparing with known cases and literature on cross-reactivity.
steps.searchable_summary: validate diagnosis using literature and known cases

methods.method_name: Study of Sensitizing Pollens and Triggering Foods
methods.description: Investigation of the most frequently involved sensitizing pollens and triggering foods in oral allergy syndrome.

steps.description: Survey allergists to gather data on common sensitizing pollens and triggering foods.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct survey with allergists for pollen and food data

steps.description: Analyze survey data to identify the most frequently involved sensitizing pollens and triggering foods.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze survey data for common allergens
outputs: list of common sensitizing pollens and foods
